message:
feat(zone): add z:project:<name> zone family + tag:fcp-proj-<name> Tailscale mapping with reserved-prefix protection

Adds a new zone-id family `z:project:<name>` (and its matching
Tailscale tag family `tag:fcp-proj-<name>`) so project-scoped zones
can be expressed as first-class identifiers alongside the existing
`z:owner` / `z:private` / `z:work` / `z:community` / `z:public`
canonical zones. The mapping is coordinated across three crates so
all three sides (ZoneId parser, Tailscale tag converter, conformance
vectors) agree.

**`fcp-core/src/capability.rs` — the ZoneId side:**

- New error variant `ZoneIdError::ReservedPrefix { prefix:
  &'static str }` so misuse can be reported with a static, grep-able
  prefix name instead of freeform strings.
- `ZoneId::to_tailscale_tag` now special-cases the
  `z:project:<name>` prefix, emitting `tag:fcp-proj-<name>` (with any
  `_` in the project name converted to `-` for Tailscale-safety);
  all other zones keep their existing behaviour.
- `ZoneId::from_tailscale_tag` gets the inverse mapping: a
  `tag:fcp-proj-<name>` input decodes back to `z:project:<name>`
  rather than the naïve `z:proj-<name>`.
- `validate_zone_id` is extended with two new checks:
  1. Any zone whose first post-prefix segment starts with `proj-`
     is rejected with `ReservedPrefix { prefix: "z:proj-" }`. This
     is what keeps an attacker from registering `z:proj-foo` and
     having it collide with the reserved `tag:fcp-proj-foo` family
     from the Tailscale side.
  2. Zones matching `z:project:<name>` route through a new helper
     `validate_project_zone_name(project)` which enforces:
     - No leading `-`
     - No trailing `-`
     - Only `[a-z0-9-]` characters
     Each violation returns `InvalidChar` with a byte index that
     already includes the `"z:project:"` prefix length, so error
     locations are absolute rather than relative to the suffix.

**`fcp-tailscale/src/tag.rs` — the TailscaleTag side:**

- Doc table in the header extended with the new row:
  `| z:project:<name> | tag:fcp-proj-<name> |`.
- `ZoneTagMapping::try_zone_to_tag` branches on whether the input
  zone starts with `z:project:`; if so, it builds `proj-<project>`
  as the tag suffix, otherwise it falls through to the existing
  `ZONE_PREFIX` strip behaviour.
- `ZoneTagMapping::tag_to_zone` picks up the inverse: if the tag's
  FCP suffix starts with `proj-`, the zone is rebuilt as
  `z:project:<project>`; otherwise the existing `{ZONE_PREFIX}<suffix>`
  format is used.
- Doc comment on the public `try_zone_to_tag` signature updated to
  reflect the new `z:<name>` OR `z:project:<name>` shape.

**`fcp-conformance/src/vectors/core.rs` — pin the contract:**

Three new tests plus an extension of the existing roundtrip loop:

- Existing `zone_id_tailscale_tag_roundtrip_for_all_canonical_zones`
  gets `"z:project:foo".parse().unwrap()` chained into the iterator,
  so the new zone family is covered by the same tag-prefix /
  roundtrip invariants as the existing zones.
- `project_zone_uses_project_tag_family`: pins both directions of
  the `z:project:foo` ↔ `tag:fcp-proj-foo` mapping, asserting the
  exact tag value on the forward direction and the exact zone
  string on the reverse.
- `abbreviated_project_zone_alias_is_rejected`: asserts that the
  hand-shortened alias `z:proj-foo` fails `ZoneId::from_str`, with
  an explanation comment noting that the collision would otherwise
  be on the `tag:fcp-proj-*` family.
- `project_zone_names_are_tailscale_tag_safe` (body included in
  the diff, not shown in this message): asserts that project names
  survive round-trip through the Tailscale tag charset without
  corruption.

Nothing about existing zone handling changes — `z:work` still maps
to `tag:fcp-work`, `z:private` still round-trips identically, etc.
The reserved-prefix guard means *new* inputs of the form `z:proj-*`
are rejected going forward, but no existing valid zone matches that
pattern, so no existing state becomes invalid.
